vision: An internal tool where engineers request production deployments and team leads approve or reject them, leaving a tamper-evident audit trail of who shipped what and who signed off.
problem: Production deploys happen ad hoc over Slack DMs. There is no consistent approval step and no record of who authorized a given release, which bites during incident review.

target_users.label: engineer
target_users.description: requests a deploy of a service to an environment

target_users.label: team lead
target_users.description: approves or rejects deploy requests for their services

goals.text: every production deploy passes through an explicit approval step
a durable audit trail records requester, approver, service, and decision
approvals are fast enough not to slow the team down

non_goals.text: building the CI/CD pipeline itself — this gates an existing pipeline, it does not replace it

constraints.text: internal only, deployed behind the company SSO gateway
small team, target 1-3 months to first use
must authenticate via the existing GitHub SSO
audit records must be durable and support concurrent approvers

budget.text: unspecified — runs on existing internal infra

success_metrics.metric: share of production deploys that went through an approval
median time from request to decision

assumptions.text: all engineers and leads already have GitHub SSO accounts
the existing deploy pipeline can be triggered by an authenticated API call

risks.text: if approvals are slow, engineers route around the tool and the audit trail has gaps
